name: Sum of phi^(n^2)
formula: I(A) = sum(phi^(n^2) for n in shells)
interpretation: Quadratic shell scaling in the phi exponent.

name: Mode-weighted phi product
formula: I(A) = prod(n * phi^n for n in shells)
interpretation: Each shell contributes its index times phi^n. Combines mode count with geometry.

name: Vandermonde-phi invariant
formula: I(A) = prod(|n_i - n_j| for i<j) * phi^(sum shells) for multi-shell; 1 for single
interpretation: Vandermonde-type discriminant times total phi depth. Measures inter-shell coupling.

name: Triangular phi sum
formula: I(A) = sum(phi^(n(n+1)/2) for n in shells)
interpretation: Triangular-number scaling in phi exponent. Natural from nested closure.

name: Lucas number product
formula: I(A) = prod(L_n for n in shells) where L_n is the n-th Lucas number
interpretation: Lucas numbers arise naturally from phi: L_n = phi^n + (-phi)^{-n}. Integer-valued.

name: Phi-weighted shell product
formula: I(A) = prod(phi^n for n in shells)
interpretation: Product of phi^n over occupied shells. Phi enters through shell geometry.

name: Phi to shell-sum
interpretation: Phi raised to the total shell index. Measures total geometric depth.

name: Closure cycle product
formula: I(A) = prod(phi^n + phi^{-n} for n in shells)
interpretation: Each shell contributes a symmetric closure cycle term. This is 2*cosh(n*ln(phi)).

name: Shell product
interpretation: Product of occupied shell indices. Measures composite complexity.

name: Fibonacci product
formula: I(A) = prod(F_n for n in shells) where F_n is the n-th Fibonacci number
interpretation: Fibonacci numbers are the integer sequence most closely tied to phi.

name: Shell-factorial × phi^shells
formula: I(A) = prod(n! * phi^n for n in shells)
interpretation: Closure complexity: each shell contributes n! permutations weighted by phi^n.

name: Phi to shell-product
interpretation: Phi raised to the product of shell indices. Exponential coupling of shell structure.

name: Phi^(sum × count)
interpretation: Total depth times mode count in phi exponent. Self-referential complexity.

name: Phi to sum-of-squares
formula: I(A) = phi^(sum(n^2 for n in shells))
interpretation: Phi raised to the sum of squared shell indices. Quadratic complexity measure.

name: Product of phi^(n^2)
formula: I(A) = prod(phi^(n^2) for n in shells)
interpretation: Quadratic phi power per shell, multiplicative. Steeper than I03.
